channel.title: SEA Policy & Capital Watch
channel.description: Policy developments and capital flows shaping Southeast Asian food systems — regulators, development banks, investors, and the architecture around them.

item.title: Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas issues first Asian central-bank guideline treating agroecological practice adoption as a distinct credit risk category
item.description: The Philippine central bank has published the first guideline by an Asian central bank treating agroecological practice adoption as a distinct credit risk category within its supervisory framework, effective Q3 2026. The guideline directs Philippine banks to reflect agroecological status of borrower operations in internal capital assessment, creating a structural bias toward smallholder-centred lending. BSP officials said the move was driven by portfolio concentration analysis showing material transition-risk exposure in monoculture lending books.

item.title: FAIRR launches first Asian agroecology-weighted protein producer index with 24 institutional investors signed on
item.description: The FAIRR Initiative has launched the first Asian agroecology-weighted protein producer index, with 24 institutional investors representing approximately $2.1 trillion in combined assets under management signing on as baseline users at launch. The index explicitly penalises producers with high exposure to industrial feed systems and rewards those integrating cooperative-origin sourcing and agroecological practice. FAIRR has framed the launch as a structural response to transition lock-in across the region's protein sector.

item.title: Bank of Thailand adds agroecological transition category to sustainable finance taxonomy
item.description: The Bank of Thailand has added an agroecological transition category to its sustainable finance taxonomy, separating it from the existing climate-smart agriculture classification. Regulated banks must align disclosures with the new taxonomy by end-2026, with examinations referencing taxonomy alignment beginning in the first half of 2027. The move creates Thailand's first formal regulatory distinction between productivity-led and agroecological transition pathways.

item.title: UOB prices $85m Indonesian smallholder palm oil facility against cooperative membership milestones
item.description: United Overseas Bank has closed an $85 million Indonesian smallholder palm oil lending facility priced explicitly against cooperative-membership milestones, a first for regional commercial banking. Borrowers receive stepped pricing reductions as an increasing share of sourced volume flows through registered farmer cooperatives. UOB executives described the structure as a deliberate attempt to put system finance at the cooperative tier where it has historically been absent.

item.title: MAS publishes revised transition finance taxonomy separating agroecology from climate-smart agriculture
item.description: The Monetary Authority of Singapore has published its revised transition finance taxonomy, for the first time separating agroecological and regenerative pathways from productivity-oriented climate-smart agriculture. Financial institutions have an 18-month compliance runway, with MAS indicating examinations will reference taxonomy alignment from Q4 2027. The taxonomic split has been called for by SEA campaigners for several years and is expected to influence cross-border lending frameworks across the region.

item.title: Bank Indonesia launches $140m rural credit programme prioritising farmer-led cooperatives
item.description: Bank Indonesia has launched a $140 million rural credit programme targeting farmer-led cooperatives across Java and Sulawesi, with preferential interest terms for cooperatives scoring above a defined smallholder-equity threshold. First disbursements are scheduled for May, with 23 cooperative borrowers identified in the initial tranche. The programme's design is notable for disbursing directly to cooperatives rather than via corporate off-taker intermediaries.

item.title: ASEAN technical working group circulates draft regional framework for agroecology adoption
item.description: An ASEAN technical working group has circulated a draft regional framework for agroecology adoption to member-state agriculture ministries, opening a 90-day comment period. The draft proposes shared indicators for transition progress and a non-binding mutual-recognition mechanism for cooperative certification. Ministerial endorsement is being targeted at the mid-year summit in Jakarta, though member-state positions diverge on scope.

item.title: Thailand's Rice Department proposes subsidy reform favouring low-input cultivation methods
item.description: Thailand's Rice Department has proposed a restructuring of existing rice subsidies to favour low-input and agroecological cultivation methods, with an internal pilot covering three northeastern provinces. The proposal is in cabinet review and could move to public consultation later this quarter; farmer-union responses to the draft have been mixed, reflecting concerns about transition timelines.

item.title: Indonesia reviews nitrogen fertiliser subsidy programme with soil-health scenario in play
item.description: Indonesia's Ministry of Agriculture has opened a formal review of the country's long-standing nitrogen fertiliser subsidy programme, with Ministry of Finance representation on the review panel. One of the scenarios under consideration redirects a portion of the subsidy toward soil-health inputs and cooperative-tier organic fertiliser supply. Observers describe visible ministerial tension over whether reform should be framed as transition finance or fiscal consolidation.

item.title: Aquaspark closes $280m Fund III with 45 per cent Southeast Asian mandate
item.description: Singapore-based impact investor Aquaspark has closed its third aquaculture fund at $280 million, with around 45 per cent of the mandate earmarked for Southeast Asia. Partners describe a deliberate tilt toward smallholder-clustered supply models and away from vertically integrated industrial farms, with the first four investments expected to close in the second half of the year.

item.title: AVPN convenes Asia Agroecology Funders Collaborative with 14 founding members
item.description: The Asia Venture Philanthropy Network has convened an Asia Agroecology Funders Collaborative with 14 founding members pooling grant and concessional capital. The collaborative's stated goal is to move beyond project-by-project funding and toward system finance for cooperative and smallholder-centred infrastructure across the region, with a first call for proposals expected in Q3.

item.title: DBS signals reduced appetite for industrial livestock financing across Southeast Asia
item.description: DBS Bank has told institutional clients it will reduce its lending exposure to large-scale industrial livestock operations across Southeast Asia over the next 36 months, citing physical climate and regulatory-transition risk. The bank has not committed to redirecting freed capacity toward agroecological alternatives, but the announcement is being read as an implicit acknowledgement of transition lock-in in the sector.

item.title: State Bank of Vietnam opens consultation on green agricultural lending framework
item.description: The State Bank of Vietnam has opened a consultation on a proposed green agricultural lending framework, inviting comment from commercial banks and farmer-union bodies. Draft language references both cooperative-tier origination and agroecological transition, though industry observers note the framework remains broad and non-binding at this stage.

item.title: Singapore opens $30m alternative-protein R&D grant round emphasising precision fermentation
item.description: Singapore's Enterprise Development agency has opened a $30 million grant round for alternative-protein R&D, with priority given to precision fermentation and cultivated-meat scale-up projects. The programme explicitly frames alternative proteins as central to Singapore's climate-smart protein strategy and to the country's regional export ambitions.

item.title: European Commission finalises revised dairy import tariff schedule
item.description: The European Commission has finalised a revised dairy import tariff schedule affecting trade with several non-EU jurisdictions. The changes are focused on North American and Oceania flows and carry no direct application to Southeast Asian dairy exporters, though regional analysts will track secondary market effects.
